channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Top Energy Officials Arrested as Probe into Substandard Fuel Imports Intensifies
item.description: Kenya has seen a major shake-up in the energy sector as four top government officials, including Petroleum PS Mohamed Liban, EPRA Director General Daniel Kiptoo, and Kenya Pipeline Company MD Joe Sang...

item.title: Toronto Shen Yun Performances Cancelled After Threats Deemed Non-Credible
item.description: Organisers of the Shen Yun performing arts show in Toronto reported that a series of threats, which police assessed as non-credible, led to the cancellation of performances scheduled for April 1-5. Th...

item.title: Bill C-22 proposes new requirements for companies to assist police and CSIS in accessing digital data
item.description: The Liberal government has introduced Bill C-22, its second attempt to give law enforcement and intelligence agencies easier access to Canadians' digital information. Section 15 of the bill would requ...

item.title: Teen survives SUV fire in Eastern Cape crash that claims three lives
item.description: In the early hours of Good Friday, a tragic head-on collision on the R67 between Seymour and Whittlesea in the Eastern Cape claimed three lives. The crash involved a sport utility vehicle (SUV) and a ...

item.title: Artemis II Astronaut Resolves Toilet Malfunction During Mission
item.description: During the first day of NASA's Artemis II mission, the spacecraft experienced a malfunction with its $30 million Universal Waste Management System (UWMS) due to an issue with the toilet fan. The crew,...

item.title: Quebec's Secularism Law May Restrict Public Good Friday Religious Processions
item.description: Christians across Quebec are facing new restrictions on public religious events following the recent passage of a provincial secularism law. The legislation, adopted on Thursday, expands the existing ...

item.title: Megan Thee Stallion Takes a Day to Rest After Feeling Unwell on Stage
item.description: American rapper Megan Thee Stallion recently worried fans when she almost collapsed during a live performance in New York. She was rushed to hospital, prompting friends and her hairstylist to ask foll...

item.title: Laura Benanti humorously overlooked by theater teens during flight
item.description: Tony Award-winning actress Laura Benanti experienced a humorous and slightly humbling moment during a recent flight. Surrounded by a group of theater-loving teenagers, Benanti realized that despite he...

item.title: Albanese’s Easter Address Focuses on Spending Amid Broader Issues
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese delivered a national address over Easter that struck many as unusually light and somewhat odd. Rather than addressing pressing national challenges or offering concrete ...

item.title: Four Toddlers Stabbed to Death in Kampala Nursery, Suspect Arrested
item.description: In a tragic event at a nursery school in Kampala, Uganda, four toddlers, aged between two and three years, were fatally stabbed. The victims included three boys and one girl, who all died instantly. T...

item.title: Billy Slater comments on the possibility of Bronson Xerri leaving the Bulldogs
item.description: NRL legend Billy Slater has weighed in on the future of Bronson Xerri at the Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs, suggesting that a departure from the club might be on the horizon. Speaking recently, Slater...

item.title: One-way traffic trial to be conducted around KBR Park on Sunday due to flyover construction
item.description: On Sunday, a trial run for one-way traffic will be implemented around KBR Park in Hyderabad between 11 a.m. and 1 p.m., owing to ongoing construction work for a steel flyover and underpass under the H...

item.title: Israel passes law allowing execution of Palestinian detainees
item.description: Israel has enacted a law permitting the execution of Palestinian prisoners, marking a stark legal shift from covert practices to codified state-sanctioned killing. The law comes amidst a system where ...

item.title: JPMorgan Reduces Stake in Indivior While Other Funds Increase Investments
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. reduced its holdings in Indivior PLC by 47% during the third quarter, selling 183,798 shares and retaining 207,588 shares valued at approximately $5.01 million, representing about...

item.title: Canadian TV highlights skilled trades and essential workers
item.description: Recent Canadian television programming is shifting focus from the glamorous lifestyles of the rich and famous to the everyday lives of skilled tradespeople. Crave’s comedic series The Trades returned ...

item.title: Risks and Safety Concerns When Nuclear Facilities Are Targeted in Conflict
item.description: As US and Israeli airstrikes target Iran's nuclear facilities, experts emphasize that the primary danger lies not in the immediate explosion but in potential failures of critical safety systems. Moder...

item.title: Invasive Meningococcal Disease Cases Rise in Canada After Years of Decline
item.description: Canada is experiencing a significant rise in cases of invasive meningococcal disease (IMD), a rare but potentially fatal bacterial infection. Federal and provincial data indicate that annual cases hav...

item.title: Iran cautions UN against escalating tensions over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran don come out to warn the UN Security Council say make dem no try any provocative action concerning the Strait of Hormuz. This na ahead of a planned vote wey suppose decide whether to send force t...

item.title: Gulf countries plan pipelines to reduce reliance on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Gulf nations are reviewing plans for new pipelines that would bypass the Strait of Hormuz due to concerns over potential disruptions by Iran. The strait is a critical chokepoint through which a large ...

item.title: Albanese urged to prioritise a long-term fuel strategy amid current crisis
item.description: The Albanese government faces mounting pressure to establish a long-term fuel security plan amid the ongoing fuel crisis. While the government has taken short-term steps, including a cut to the fuel e...
